question: The plot shows training hours vs test score. Based on the chart data, which statement is correct?

options.A: Students with around 20 training hours achieve the highest test scores.
options.B: Students with either very low (0–5) or very high (35–40) training hours achieve higher test scores than those with around 20 hours.
options.C: Test scores stay roughly constant regardless of training hours.
options.D: Students with around 15 training hours achieve the lowest test scores.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the y-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
